rationale: The strongest path so far is late quant-aware tuning plus selective late-decoder FP16 rescue. This run keeps compounding that: it resets Muon and Adam exactly when the first quant-aware roundtrip starts so stale optimizer state does not fight the quantized basin, and it spends a bit more size budget on the most quantization-sensitive tail blocks by default. The change is cheap in wallclock, directly targets final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_bpb, and stays under the 1500-line limit.
title: Quant-Aware Phase Reset With Deeper Tail FP16 Rescue
